title: Testing the generalization of neural representations

frameworks.name: multivariate pattern analysis
frameworks.one_line: decoder-based pattern generalization across time and contexts in mass neural signals

frameworks.name: pattern-based encoding models
frameworks.one_line: comparable encoding approach to assess representational similarity

core_question: What conclusions about neural representations are valid when pattern generalization is detected in mass signals like LFP, EEG, MEG, or fMRI?
key_assumptions: mass signals reflect mixtures of underlying neural populations
decoder generalization is the standard probe for representational similarity
signal mixing and measurement dependencies confound interpretation
manipulations: signal mixing structure and measurement dependencies in mass-signal models
key_findings: significant cross-context pattern generalization can arise even when true representations are orthogonal
signal mixing and dependencies between measurements drive spurious generalization
interpretations: an accurate estimate of expected generalization under identical representations enables meaningful hypothesis tests
the authors provide such a baseline to assess similarity and differences across time and contexts
